nl_query:
Schema: fields: ActionType, AntivirusScan, DeviceEvents, DeviceId
Get antivirus scan events, including completed and cancelled scans on device "16352fa21f135"
cql_query:
DeviceEvents 
| where ActionType startswith "AntivirusScan" 
| where DeviceId == "16352fa21f135"

nl_query:
Schema: fields: DeviceId, DeviceName, DeviceNetworkInfo, IPAddress, IPAddresses
List all devices that have been assigned IP address 192.168.1.5
cql_query:
DeviceNetworkInfo
| mv-expand parse_json(IPAddresses)
| extend IPAddress = IPAddresses.IPAddress
| where IPAddress == "192.168.30.1"
| summarize by DeviceId, DeviceName
schema_context: fields: DeviceId, DeviceName, DeviceNetworkInfo, IPAddress, IPAddresses

nl_query:
Schema: fields: AccountName, ActionType, IdentityLogonEvents, LogonSuccess
total count of distinct users who have logged in more than their average login in the last 7 days
cql_query:
IdentityLogonEvents
| where ActionType == 'LogonSuccess'
| summarize count() by AccountName, bin_at(Timestamp, 7d, now())
| summarize avg(count_) by AccountName
| join IdentityLogonEvents on AccountName
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ActionType == 'LogonSuccess'
| summarize count() by AccountName, avg_count_
| where count_ > avg_count_
| count

nl_query:
Schema: fields: BytesReceived, BytesSent, VMConnection
Count VM connections per direction, bytes sent, and bytes received?
cql_query:
VMConnection
| summarize Count=count() by Direction, BytesSent, BytesReceived
| sort by Count

nl_query: Cloud Data Exfiltration IOMs: This query outputs all identified indicators of misconfigurations (IOMs) related to data exfiltration.

Schema: ProcessRollup2: aid, ComputerName, ImageFileName, CommandLine, ParentBaseFileName, ParentCommandLine; fields: CommandLine, ImageFileName, ParentImageFileName, UserIdentity, UserName
Suspicious PowerShell Execution: This query identifies suspicious PowerShell execution patterns, including encoded commands and unusual parent processes, which could indicate malicious activity.
cql_query:
#event_simpleName=ProcessRollup2 ImageFileName=/\\powershell\\.exe/i
| CommandLine=/\s-[eE^]{1,2}[nN][cC][oO][dD][eE][mM][aA][nN][dD^]+\s/i
| join({#event_simpleName=UserIdentity}, field=AuthenticationID, include=[UserName])
| table([aid, UserName, ParentImageFileName, ImageFileName, CommandLine])
schema_context: ProcessRollup2: aid, ComputerName, ImageFileName, CommandLine, ParentBaseFileName, ParentCommandLine; fields: CommandLine, ImageFileName, ParentImageFileName, UserIdentity, UserName

nl_query:
Schema: fields: AADNonInteractiveUserSignInLogs, ConditionalAccessStatus, IPAddress, ResultType, TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName
Help me identify which users attempted to bypass conditional access rule in Azure Active Directory during the last 12 days and tell me what Ipaddress they were using
cql_query:
AADNonInteractiveUserSignInLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(12d)
| where ConditionalAccessStatus =~ "failure"
| where ResultType != 0
| summarize  make_set(UserPrincipalName),make_set(Location) by IPAddress
schema_context: fields: AADNonInteractiveUserSignInLogs, ConditionalAccessStatus, IPAddress, ResultType, TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName

nl_query:
Schema: fields: AADServicePrincipalSignInLogs, AppId, AuthenticationProcessingDetails, IPAddress, LocationDetails, ServicePrincipalId, ServicePrincipalName, TimeGenerated
For last 7 days, list all MS Apps and project  TimeGenerated,Authkey,Authvalue,ServicePrincipalName,ServicePrincipalId, AppId, IPAddress,country,city,state
cql_query:
AADServicePrincipalSignInLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| extend locationdetails = parse_json(LocationDetails)
| extend country = locationdetails.countryOrRegion
| extend city = locationdetails.city
| extend state = locationdetails.state
| extend Authdetails = parse_json(AuthenticationProcessingDetails)
| extend Authdetails = Authdetails.[0]
| extend Authkey = Authdetails.key
| extend Authvalue = tostring(Authdetails.value)
| project TimeGenerated,Authkey,Authvalue,ServicePrincipalName,ServicePrincipalId, AppId, IPAddress,country,city,state
schema_context: fields: AADServicePrincipalSignInLogs, AppId, AuthenticationProcessingDetails, IPAddress, LocationDetails, ServicePrincipalId, ServicePrincipalName, TimeGenerated

nl_query:
Schema: fields: AADManagedIdentitySignInLogs, AADServicePrincipalSignInLogs, AccountCustomEntity, CorrelationId, IPAddress, IPCustomEntity, ResourceDisplayName, ResultType
Show 100 successful managed identity sign ins join with AADServicePrincipalSinginlogs where logins is unsuccessful on ServicePrincipalName Columns with more than one application
cql_query:
AADManagedIdentitySignInLogs 
| where ResultType == 0
| project TimeGenerated, Category, CorrelationId, ServicePrincipalId,ServicePrincipalName, ResourceDisplayName, IPAddress, Location
| join kind=inner (AADServicePrincipalSignInLogs
| where ResultType != "0"
| summarize applicationCount = dcount(ResourceDisplayName), applicationSet = makeset(ResourceDisplayName) by TimeGenerated, ServicePrincipalName, IPAddress, ResultType
| where applicationCount >= 1
| extend TimeGenerated, AccountCustomEntity = ServicePrincipalName, IPCustomEntity = IPAddress, ResultType ) on $left.ServicePrincipalName == $right.ServicePrincipalName
| limit 100
schema_context: fields: AADManagedIdentitySignInLogs, AADServicePrincipalSignInLogs, AccountCustomEntity, CorrelationId, IPAddress, IPCustomEntity, ResourceDisplayName, ResultType

nl_query:
Schema: fields: DeviceNetworkEvents, InitiatingProcessFileName, RemoteUrl
Get network events initiated by installer.msi where url contains contonso.com or filedrop between 2022-10-05 18:42 and 2022-10-06 1:59
cql_query:
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp between (datetime("2022-10-05T18:42Z") .. datetime("2022-10-06T01:59Z"))
| where InitiatingProcessFileName == "installer.msi"
| where RemoteUrl contains "contonso.com" or RemoteUrl contains "filedrop"

nl_query:
Schema: fields: CommandLine, ImageFileName, ProcessBlocked
LOLBin Regsvr32: This query detects the use of Regsvr32 when it has loaded scrobj.dll.
cql_query:
in(#event_simpleName, values=["ProcessRollup2","ProcessBlocked"])
| event_platform=Win
| ImageFileName=/regsvr32.exe/i CommandLine=/scrobj.dll/i CommandLine=/i:/i

nl_query:
Schema: fields: DeviceFileEvents, FileName, FolderPath, InitiatingProcessVersionInfoOriginalFileName
Get events for installer.msi where folderpath ends with "ambigublue" exlcuding processes originally named powershell.exe
cql_query:
DeviceFileEvents
| where FileName =~ "installer.msi"  
| where FolderPath endswith "ambigublue"
| where InitiatingProcessVersionInfoOriginalFileName <> "powershell.exe"

nl_query:
Schema: fields: BrowserExtensionId, BrowserExtensionName, BrowserName, ExtensionName, InstalledBrowserExtension, TotalEndpoints

schema_context: fields: BrowserExtensionId, BrowserExtensionName, BrowserName, ExtensionName, InstalledBrowserExtension, TotalEndpoints

nl_query:
Schema: fields: OsVersionInfo, ProductName
Evaluate Operating System Prevalence: This query counts how many Windows endpoints are running each OS version (like Windows 10, Windows 11, etc.) in your CrowdStrike environment. It groups endpoints by their current OS product name and returns the count for each version.
cql_query:
#event_simpleName=OsVersionInfo event_platform=Win
| groupby(aid, function=selectLast([ProductName]))
| groupBy([ProductName], function=stats([count(aid, as="endpointCount")]))

nl_query:
Schema: fields: DeviceNetworkEvents
Show 10000 oldest network connection details events between 2022-10-06 01:39 and 2022-10-06 01:44
cql_query:
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp between (datetime("2022-10-06T01:39Z") .. datetime("2022-10-06T01:44Z"))
| top 10000 by Timestamp asc

nl_query:
Schema: fields: UrlClickEvents
Dislay 10000 url related event triggered from 2022-10-06 01:55 to 2022-10-06 01:58
cql_query:
UrlClickEvents
| where Timestamp between(datetime("2022-10-06T01:55Z")..datetime("2022-10-06T01:58Z"))
| limit  10000

nl_query:
Schema: fields: DeviceFileEvents, DeviceImageLoadEvents, FileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine
Show image load events for files ending in "crypto.dll" initiated by powershell and files ending in "crypto.msi" that were accessed for the first time in 24hrs.
cql_query:
union (
    DeviceImageLoadEvents
    | where FileName endswith "crypto.dll"
       | where InitiatingProcessCommandLine contains "powershell"
), (
    DeviceFileEvents 
    | where FileName endswith "crypto.msi"
    | summarize argmin(Timestamp, *) by FileName, SHA256, SHA1, bin(Timestamp, 1d)
 )

Schema: fields: AzureLocation, AzureResourceGroup, AzureResourceName, VMComputer

VMComputer
| where AzureLocation == "westeurope"

nl_query:
Schema: fields: AccountName, ActionType, DeviceEvents, DeviceName, FileName, FolderPath, UsbDriveMount
Summarize usb mount events by device, account, and file location
cql_query:
DeviceEvents
| where ActionType contains "UsbDriveMount"
| summarize by FileName, FolderPath , DeviceName , AccountName
schema_context: fields: AccountName, ActionType, DeviceEvents, DeviceName, FileName, FolderPath, UsbDriveMount

nl_query:
Schema: NetworkConnectIP4: aid, ComputerName, RemoteAddressIP4, RemotePort, LocalAddressIP4, LocalPort; fields: RemotePort
Rare Remote Ports in Network Connections: The query analyzes IPv4 network connection events, counts occurrences per remote port, calculates their percentage of total connections, and lists only ports representing less than 10% of the traffic.
cql_query:
#event_simpleName=NetworkConnectIP4
| groupBy([RemotePort], function=count(as=count), limit=max) 
| [sum(count, as=total), sort(field=RemotePort, order=ascending, limit=20000)] 
| percent := 100 * (count / total) 
| drop([total]) 
| percent < 10
schema_context: NetworkConnectIP4: aid, ComputerName, RemoteAddressIP4, RemotePort, LocalAddressIP4, LocalPort; fields: RemotePort

nl_query:
Schema: fields: StatusCode, StorageFileLogs

| where StatusCode == -1073741772
| where Location == "centralus"

nl_query:

nl_query:
Schema: DnsRequest: aid, ComputerName, DomainName, RequestType, RespondingDnsServer, ImageFileName; fields: ContextBaseFileName, DnsRequest, DomainName, HostCount
Detect Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) Tools over DNS: This query identifies the presence or execution of common RMM utilities (e.g., AnyDesk, TeamViewer, ConnectWise, ScreenConnect, Splashtop). While these tools are legitimate and widely used for IT administration, adversaries often abuse them as “living-off-the-land” remote access backdoors. Because they operate under the guise of trusted software and can blend with normal activity, malicious use of RMM tools may bypass traditional security controls, enabling persistence, data exfiltration, or hands-on-keyboard attacks.
cql_query:
#event_simpleName=DnsRequest
| DomainName=/anydesk\.com|action1\.com|beamyourscreen\.com|snapview\.de|rustdesk\.com|fleetdeck\.io|tailscale\.com|dwservice\.net|secure\.logmein\.com|teamviewer\.com|screenconnect\.com|fixme\.it|n-able\.com|domotz\.com|datto\.com|level\.io|itarian\.com|pulseway\.com|zoho\.com|manageengine\.com|bomgarcloud\.com|bomgar\.com|zabbix\.com/i
| groupBy([DomainName],function=[collect(ContextBaseFileName), count(aid,distinct=true,as=HostCount)])
| sort(HostCount,order=asc)
schema_context: DnsRequest: aid, ComputerName, DomainName, RequestType, RespondingDnsServer, ImageFileName; fields: ContextBaseFileName, DnsRequest, DomainName, HostCount

nl_query:
Schema: fields: DeviceInfo, DeviceName
find device names that contain surface
cql_query:
DeviceInfo
| where DeviceName contains "surface"

nl_query:
Schema: fields: AuthenticationType, GetBlobServiceProperties, ListBlobs, OperationName, StorageBlobLogs
Show all blob list and properties operations from users authenticated with oauth
cql_query:
StorageBlobLogs
| where AuthenticationType == 'OAuth'
| where not(OperationName has_any('GetBlobServiceProperties', 'ListBlobs'))
schema_context: fields: AuthenticationType, GetBlobServiceProperties, ListBlobs, OperationName, StorageBlobLogs

nl_query:
Schema: NetworkConnectIP4: aid, ComputerName, RemoteAddressIP4, RemotePort, LocalAddressIP4, LocalPort; ProcessRollup2: aid, ComputerName, ImageFileName, CommandLine, ParentBaseFileName, ParentCommandLine; fields: CommandLine, ComputerName, ContextProcessId, FileName, ImageFileName, RemotePort, TargetProcessId, UserName
External Connectons with Process
cql_query:
#event_simpleName=NetworkConnectIP4 aid=?aid ComputerName=?Computername RemoteAddressIP4=?RemoteIP 
| !cidr(RemoteAddressIP4, subnet=["10.0.0.0/8","192.168.0.0/16","172.16.0.0/12","127.0.0.0/8"])
| join({#event_simpleName=ProcessRollup2  FileName=?Processname }, field=[ContextProcessId],key=TargetProcessId, include=[FileName, UserName,ImageFileName, RemoteAddressIP4, RemotePort,CommandLine], mode=left)
| groupBy(UserName, function=collect([FileName, UserName, ImageFileName, RemoteIP, RPort, CommandLine]))
| sort([_count], order=asc)
schema_context: NetworkConnectIP4: aid, ComputerName, RemoteAddressIP4, RemotePort, LocalAddressIP4, LocalPort; ProcessRollup2: aid, ComputerName, ImageFileName, CommandLine, ParentBaseFileName, ParentCommandLine; fields: CommandLine, ComputerName, ContextProcessId, FileName, ImageFileName, RemotePort, TargetProcessId, UserName

nl_query:
Schema: ProcessRollup2: aid, ComputerName, ImageFileName, CommandLine, ParentBaseFileName, ParentCommandLine; fields: CmdLinePrefix, CommandLine, DecodedString, EncodedCommand, EncodedString, ImageFileName, SubCmdLinePrefix, SubDecodedString
Detect and Decode Base64-Encoded PowerShell Commands - http: The query identifies Windows PowerShell executions using encoded commands, extracts and decodes Base64 payloads (including nested encodings), counts occurrences and unique hosts, and outputs decoded command content for analysis of potentially obfuscated activity.
cql_query:
#event_simpleName=ProcessRollup2 event_platform=Win ImageFileName=/.*\\powershell\.exe/
| CommandLine=/.*\s+\-(e|encoded|encodedcommand|enc)\s+.*/
| length("CommandLine", as="cmdLength")
| groupby([CommandLine], function=stats([count(aid, distinct=true, as="uniqueEndpointCount"), count(aid, as="executionCount")]), limit=max)
| EncodedString := splitString(field=CommandLine, by="-e* ", index=1)
| CmdLinePrefix := splitString(field=CommandLine, by="-e* ", index=0)
| DecodedString := base64Decode(EncodedString, charset="UTF-16LE")
// Look for encoded messages in the decoded message and decode those too.
| case {
  DecodedString = /encoded/i
  | SubEncodedString := splitString(field=DecodedString, by="-EncodedCommand ", index=1)
  | SubCmdLinePrefix := splitString(field=EncodedString, by="-EncodedCommand ", index=0)
  | SubDecodedString := base64Decode(SubEncodedString, charset="UTF-16LE");
  *
}
| DecodedString=/.*https?\:\/\/.*/
| table([executionCount, uniqueEndpoitnCount, DecodedString, CommandLine])
| sort(executionCount, order=desc)
schema_context: ProcessRollup2: aid, ComputerName, ImageFileName, CommandLine, ParentBaseFileName, ParentCommandLine; fields: CmdLinePrefix, CommandLine, DecodedString, EncodedCommand, EncodedString, ImageFileName, SubCmdLinePrefix, SubDecodedString

nl_query:
Schema: fields: AccountDisplayName, AccountName, IdentityInfo, JobTitle, TimeGenerated
For last 7 day, show me all user identity info and make a table of AccountUPN,AccountName,AccountDisplayName,JobTitle,City,Country
cql_query:
IdentityInfo
    | where TimeGenerated > ago (7d)
    | summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by AccountUPN
    | project AccountUPN,AccountName,AccountDisplayName,JobTitle,City,Country
schema_context: fields: AccountDisplayName, AccountName, IdentityInfo, JobTitle, TimeGenerated

| project UserInsights["AccountDisplayName"]

nl_query:
Schema: fields: DeviceId, DeviceProcessEvents, InitiatingProcessParentFileName, ProcessCommandLine, ProcessIntegrityLevel, ReportId
Get timestamp, device and report id for svchost and outlook process events that include cmd.exe, beacon or blue in the command line and have high or system ingerity level
cql_query:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where InitiatingProcessParentFileName has_any ('svchost', 'outlook')
| where  ProcessIntegrityLevel in  ('High','System')
| where  ProcessCommandLine has_all ('cmd.exe','beacon','blue')
| project Timestamp, DeviceId, ReportId
schema_context: fields: DeviceId, DeviceProcessEvents, InitiatingProcessParentFileName, ProcessCommandLine, ProcessIntegrityLevel, ReportId

| where ThreatTypes has "Phish"
| where EmailActionPolicy != "Anti-phishing user impersonation"

event_type=ZeroTrustHostAssessment
| groupBy([aid], function=([selectFromMax(field="@timestamp", include=[scores.os, scores.sensor, scores.overall])]))

nl_query:
Schema: fields: ComputerName, SensorHeartbeat, SensorStateBitMap
Detect locally disabled RTR: This query identifies hosts with locally disabled RTR.
cql_query:
#event_simpleName=SensorHeartbeat
| groupBy([aid], function=selectLast([@timestamp, ComputerName, SensorStateBitMap]), limit=max)
| bitfield:extractFlags(
field=SensorStateBitMap,
 output=[
   [2, RTR_Locally_Disabled]
])
| RTR_Locally_Disabled="true"

nl_query:
Schema: fields: AlertId, AlertInfo
show alert info for id 9983712fbafd12f412f4

nl_query:
Schema: ProcessRollup2: aid, ComputerName, ImageFileName, CommandLine, ParentBaseFileName, ParentCommandLine; fields: CommandLine, FileName, ImageFileName, ShortFile, TargetProcessId
List of attachments sent from Outlook
cql_query:
#event_simpleName=ProcessRollup2
| CommandLine=/content.outlook/i
| aid=?aid
| ImageFileName=/(\/|\\)(?<FileName>\w*\.?\w*)$/
| FileName=/(winword|excel|powerpnt)\.exe/i
| CommandLine=/Outlook\\(?<ShortFile>\w*\\.*)$/i
| table([@timestamp, aid, TargetProcessId, ShortFile, CommandLine], limit=1000)
schema_context: ProcessRollup2: aid, ComputerName, ImageFileName, CommandLine, ParentBaseFileName, ParentCommandLine; fields: CommandLine, FileName, ImageFileName, ShortFile, TargetProcessId

nl_query:
Schema: fields: AWSGuardDuty, ActivityType, ConsoleLoginSuccess, IAMUser, InstanceCredentialExfiltration, MaliciousIPCaller, TorIPCaller, UnauthorizedAccess
Show me alerts from AWSGuardDuty for unauthorized access
cql_query:
AWSGuardDuty
| where ActivityType has_any ("UnauthorizedAccess:IAMUser/TorIPCaller", "UnauthorizedAccess:IAMUser/MaliciousIPCaller.Custom", 
 "UnauthorizedAccess:IAMUser/InstanceCredentialExfiltration.OutsideAWS", "UnauthorizedAccess:IAMUser/InstanceCredentialExfiltration.InsideAWS",
"UnauthorizedAccess:IAMUser/ConsoleLoginSuccess.B","UnauthorizedAccess:IAMUser/MaliciousIPCaller")
schema_context: fields: AWSGuardDuty, ActivityType, ConsoleLoginSuccess, IAMUser, InstanceCredentialExfiltration, MaliciousIPCaller, TorIPCaller, UnauthorizedAccess
